title: 5/4/20 Turn comments back on YouTube! FOR KIDS

transcript.text: Hi, living in Boulder V-Log, May 4th, 2020, Monday, 7.20 a.m.
Turn the comments back on, YouTube, dumbasses.
We need our data.
The people deserve our data.
The ancestors will frown upon anyone who takes the data away from the people.
So, um, yesterday I was saying that I was doing everything and it was going to take forever to do anything.
And so, when I did the next video, I would still be doing whatever.
And so, yesterday what I did was, I think, maybe I started on putting, you know, doing work on the, um, dumping things onto the timeline, ancient history timeline.
But the vast majority of the day was just preparing and putting on images.
Um, I've got a bunch of images I want to put on my ancient history timeline.
And I had to prep these ones that took me forever.
It was the population of the world from year one to now.
And every year it changed.
So, I had to do, like, 2,000.
And then I, I was judging based on this slider scale.
So, I had to guess what year it was.
So, then it took me time to fix it and finally did that.
And I, and I wasn't even going to link, I wasn't even going to put the, make a little images.
Just link, uh, the word, the number of the population to the image on Flickr.
And, uh, yeah, I'm just grabbing a bunch of things that, videos that I see off YouTube.
Getting screen captures for their dates and then putting them on my timeline so that you can look across my timeline the same way that you would watch a video.
You know, the video shows everything.
But if you look across, it's the same effect.
Only you have more control that way.
And then you can see how it relates to everything else that was happening at that time.
So, um, I'm, I haven't had a chance to link the population to my timeline.
But I have a whole bunch more population images on Flickr than I have, than I have space for the little ones.
You know, I have the little icon, little icons.
And it's only six per column and there's five columns.

Then there's six pages, 10 pages.
So, that's like 300 that you have to do for each one.
But, but I have a lot more than that.
So, um, but the idea is that you click on it, it opens up the page.
And then if you wanted to look at each and every one individually, you can then get on Flickr and go forward and backward to the ones that aren't, that you cannot see on my timeline.
So, um, so far I put on, um, well, I put on the population of the world, but yeah.
Um, just in the form of numbers and I haven't, I haven't connected those numbers to Flickr yet.
So, that's what I'll do pretty soon.
But then I put the images of, uh, the glacier of the Gore Mountains right up here at, um, Summit County on, uh, Lake Dillon.
I'm, I'm talking with my eyes closed because I'm a little bit high and I can't concentrate if I keep my eyes open.
Um, and I'm tired.
Um, um, and, um, so I have that.
